Types already defined at MF1.8 (Core, Custom, Lookalike, Advantage+) are cited and extended, not redefined.

### MF5.2.1 Core Audience

- See MF1.8.1 for the base definition. **Purpose:** Prospect entirely new users via manually specified demographic/interest/behavior criteria. **Construction:** Manual selection within Ads Manager (MF3.3.1). **Strengths:** No first-party data prerequisite; full advertiser control. **Weaknesses:** No system-driven optimization of the population itself; risk of over-narrowing (MF3.3.1's Common Mistakes). **Typical Performance:** Generally the least efficient of the four base types once sufficient first-party data exists to support the others. **Ideal Objectives:** All, especially early-stage Businesses with no Pixel/Customer data yet. **Ideal Funnel Stage:** Top of funnel (cold prospecting). **Best Industries:** Universal, particularly valuable pre-data-maturity. **Risks:** Over-narrowing (MF5.3.23's Narrowing risk); stereotype-driven criteria selection producing poor real-world match. **Optimization Rules:** Prefer broader criteria plus a strong Optimization Goal (MF3.5) over heavy manual narrowing, per current platform guidance.

### MF5.2.2 Saved Audience

- **Purpose:** A named, reusable Core Audience configuration (MF5.2.1) saved for repeated deployment across multiple Ad Sets without re-entering criteria each time.
- **Construction:** Save a completed Core Audience criteria set under a named configuration for future reuse.
- **Strengths:** Operational efficiency and configuration consistency across Ad Sets/Campaigns.
- **Weaknesses:** Risk of staleness — a Saved Audience's criteria can go unreviewed for extended periods even as market conditions or Business understanding evolves.
- **Typical Performance:** Identical to the underlying Core Audience configuration it saves; introduces no independent performance characteristic.
- **Ideal Objectives:** All, wherever a Core Audience configuration will be reused.
- **Ideal Funnel Stage:** Same as the underlying Core Audience use case.
- **Best Industries:** Universal, particularly valuable for agencies managing many similar Ad Sets across clients or Campaigns.
- **Risks:** Silent staleness — a specific governance risk this type introduces that a freshly-built Core Audience does not carry (MF5.18.10's Audit Standards should specifically re-validate Saved Audience currency).
- **Optimization Rules:** Periodically re-validate Saved Audience criteria against current Business understanding and performance data, not merely reuse indefinitely.

### MF5.2.3 Custom Audience

- See MF1.8.2 for the base definition; the full source-by-source treatment is delivered at MF5.4, per this Framework's required structure. **Purpose:** Target users with an existing, first-party-data-verified relationship to the Business. **Construction:** Built from a Pixel/Conversions API/App SDK Event rule, a Customer List upload, or on-platform engagement history. **Strengths:** Highest-relevance targeting available, since it is grounded in genuine prior interaction. **Weaknesses:** Entirely dependent on the Business's own data infrastructure maturity and volume. **Typical Performance:** Generally the strongest-converting Audience type for retargeting use cases. **Ideal Objectives:** Sales, Leads (retargeting sub-cases). **Ideal Funnel Stage:** Mid-to-bottom funnel. **Best Industries:** Universal, contingent on data infrastructure maturity. **Risks:** Staleness (MF3.3.2's Common Mistakes); privacy/compliance exposure (MF5.18.6–7). **Optimization Rules:** Verify source Event/lookback window relevance before every deployment (MF3.3.2).

### MF5.2.4 Lookalike Audience

- See MF1.8.3 for the base definition; the full treatment is delivered at MF5.5, per this Framework's required structure. **Purpose:** Prospect new users statistically resembling a chosen high-value source Audience. **Construction:** Select a source Custom Audience, target geography, and similarity percentage. **Strengths:** Combines prospecting breadth with source-Audience-informed relevance. **Weaknesses:** Quality entirely bounded by source Audience quality (MF1.8.3's Common Mistakes). **Typical Performance:** Strong middle ground between Core Audience's manual breadth and Custom Audience's retargeting precision. **Ideal Objectives:** Sales, Leads, App Promotion (prospecting phase). **Ideal Funnel Stage:** Top-to-mid funnel prospecting. **Best Industries:** Universal, contingent on adequate source Audience volume/quality. **Risks:** Source staleness; over-narrow similarity percentage causing Learning Limited (MF3.12.1). **Optimization Rules:** Start narrow (1%), expand incrementally per MF5.5's full treatment.

### MF5.2.5 Advantage+ Audience

- See MF1.8.4 for the base definition; the full treatment is delivered at MF5.6, per this Framework's required structure. **Purpose:** Delegate population definition to Meta's system, optionally guided by a soft Audience Signal. **Construction:** Enable Advantage+ Audience mode at the Ad Set level, optionally supplying a suggestion (MF3.3.4/3.3.6). **Strengths:** Current platform-recommended default; strongest Learning Phase and scaling characteristics. **Weaknesses:** Reduced granular brand-safety/manual control. **Typical Performance:** Generally strong to leading across most use cases per current platform data. **Ideal Objectives:** All. **Ideal Funnel Stage:** All, especially effective when paired with a relevant Signal for prospecting or retargeting alike. **Best Industries:** Universal. **Risks:** Weak/irrelevant Signal quality undermining system inference (MF3.3.6's Common Mistakes). **Optimization Rules:** Always supply the strongest available Signal rather than running fully signal-less where a relevant Audience exists.

### MF5.2.6 Engaged Audience

- **Purpose:** Target or exclude users who have interacted with the Business's on-platform content (Page, Instagram, video, Lead Form) without necessarily having visited an external website or app.
- **Construction:** A Custom Audience (MF1.8.2) built from on-platform engagement Event rules (e.g., "engaged with Page in last 90 days") rather than off-platform Pixel/App data.
- **Strengths:** Available even to Businesses with no website/Pixel infrastructure at all — the lowest-infrastructure-barrier Custom Audience type.
- **Weaknesses:** Generally lower purchase-intent signal strength than a website-behavior-based Custom Audience (MF5.4's Website Audience), since on-platform engagement is a lower-commitment action than an off-platform visit.
- **Typical Performance:** Moderate — stronger than cold Core Audience prospecting, weaker than Website/Pixel-based retargeting.
- **Ideal Objectives:** Engagement, Awareness retargeting, early-stage Leads nurture.
- **Ideal Funnel Stage:** Upper-to-mid funnel.
- **Best Industries:** Content-driven brands, community-oriented businesses, businesses without mature website tracking.
- **Risks:** Overlap with Website Audiences if a user engages on-platform and off-platform similarly, risking the Audience Overlap pattern (MF5.10) if run in parallel Ad Sets.
- **Optimization Rules:** Segment by specific engagement type (video view depth, Page interaction type) rather than a single undifferentiated "any engagement" rule, for stronger relevance.

### MF5.2.7 Website Audience

- **Purpose:** Retarget users based on their website behavior, tracked via Pixel/Conversions API (MF1.3.6–7).
- **Construction:** A Custom Audience built from a Pixel/Conversions API Event rule (Page View, View Content, Add To Cart, Purchase, etc., MF1.6).
- **Strengths:** Directly ties Audience membership to demonstrated purchase-funnel behavior, generally the strongest-intent signal available among Custom Audience sources.
- **Weaknesses:** Entirely dependent on Pixel/Conversions API implementation completeness and Matching Quality (MF1.6.17).
- **Typical Performance:** Among the strongest-converting Audience types for Sales retargeting specifically.
- **Ideal Objectives:** Sales, Leads (retargeting).
- **Ideal Funnel Stage:** Mid-to-bottom funnel, segmented further by the specific Event used (View Content = upper-mid; Add To Cart/Initiate Checkout = lower).
- **Best Industries:** E-commerce, SaaS, any website-transaction-driven business.
- **Risks:** Privacy-driven browser tracking degradation reducing Audience volume/accuracy over time (mirroring MF1.3.31's Professional Notes on Conversions API's growing importance).
- **Optimization Rules:** Segment by funnel-stage Event (MF1.6) rather than a single generic "all visitors" pool, matching lookback window to typical purchase consideration cycle length.

### MF5.2.8 Video Audience

- **Purpose:** Retarget users based on video-viewership depth (MF1.4.9), independent of website behavior.
- **Construction:** A Custom Audience built from a video watch-percentage threshold rule (e.g., "watched 50%+ of video X in last 30 days").
- **Strengths:** Available without any website/Pixel infrastructure; naturally segments by engagement depth (a 95%-watched viewer is a stronger signal than a 25%-watched one).
- **Weaknesses:** A purely attention-based signal, generally weaker purchase-intent evidence than a Website Audience's behavioral signal.
- **Typical Performance:** Moderate, strongest when combined with deep watch-percentage thresholds (75%+) rather than shallow ones.
- **Ideal Objectives:** Engagement retargeting, Awareness-to-consideration nurture sequences, Sales retargeting for video-heavy product categories.
- **Ideal Funnel Stage:** Upper-to-mid funnel.
- **Best Industries:** Media/entertainment, education, any video-content-driven marketing strategy.
- **Risks:** Shallow-threshold (e.g., 3-second view) Video Audiences carrying too little genuine intent signal to be a meaningful retargeting pool.
- **Optimization Rules:** Prefer deeper watch-percentage thresholds (50%+) for retargeting use cases; use shallow thresholds only for broad Awareness-stage remarketing.

### MF5.2.9 Page Audience

- **Purpose:** Retarget users who have interacted with the Business's Facebook Page (MF1.3.4).
- **Construction:** A Custom Audience built from Page interaction Event rules (visited Page, engaged with a post, sent a message to the Page).
- **Strengths:** Available without website infrastructure; captures broad brand-level interaction.
- **Weaknesses:** Less granular purchase-intent signal than Website Audiences; can include very passive interactions (a simple Page visit) alongside genuinely engaged ones.
- **Typical Performance:** Moderate, comparable to Engaged Audience (MF5.2.6), of which it is effectively a specific instance scoped to the Page surface.
- **Ideal Objectives:** Engagement, Awareness retargeting.
- **Ideal Funnel Stage:** Upper funnel.
- **Best Industries:** Universal, especially community/brand-engagement-driven businesses.
- **Risks:** Including overly passive interaction types diluting overall Audience intent quality.
- **Optimization Rules:** Segment by specific interaction type and recency rather than a blanket "any Page interaction" rule.

### MF5.2.10 Instagram Audience

- **Purpose:** Retarget users who have interacted with the Business's Instagram Account (MF1.3.5).
- **Construction:** A Custom Audience built from Instagram interaction Event rules (profile visit, post engagement, saved a post, direct message).
- **Strengths:** Captures a visually-engaged, often younger-skewing Audience segment distinct from Facebook Page interactors.
- **Weaknesses:** Requires a connected Instagram Account; similar passive-vs-engaged granularity concern as Page Audience (MF5.2.9).
- **Typical Performance:** Moderate, often strong for visually-driven categories given Instagram's engagement-dense format norms.
- **Ideal Objectives:** Engagement, Awareness retargeting, Sales (visually-driven categories).
- **Ideal Funnel Stage:** Upper-to-mid funnel.
- **Best Industries:** Fashion, beauty, lifestyle, visually-driven consumer brands.
- **Risks:** Overlap with Page Audience for Businesses whose Audience engages similarly across both surfaces.
- **Optimization Rules:** Segment by interaction depth (saved/shared vs. a passive profile visit) for stronger relevance.

### MF5.2.11 Messenger Audience

- **Purpose:** Retarget users who have had a Messaging Conversation (MF1.3.33) with the Business via Messenger.
- **Construction:** A Custom Audience built from Messenger conversation Event rules (opened a conversation, received a specific message tag).
- **Strengths:** Captures a conversational, often higher-intent Audience segment given the deliberate action of initiating or continuing a conversation.
- **Weaknesses:** Requires an existing Messenger presence and conversation volume to build a meaningful pool.
- **Typical Performance:** Strong for conversational-commerce/support-driven Businesses specifically.
- **Ideal Objectives:** Engagement, Leads, Sales (conversational commerce).
- **Ideal Funnel Stage:** Mid-to-bottom funnel.
- **Best Industries:** Local services, conversational-commerce brands, customer-support-driven businesses.
- **Risks:** Compliance requirements around messaging-based retargeting frequency/content, subject to platform messaging policies.
- **Optimization Rules:** Segment by conversation outcome/tag (e.g., qualified vs. unqualified) where the Business's Messenger flow supports it, rather than treating all prior conversations as equally valuable.

### MF5.2.12 WhatsApp Audience

- **Purpose:** Retarget users who have engaged with the Business via WhatsApp Business messaging.
- **Construction:** A Custom Audience built from WhatsApp conversation/interaction Event rules, requiring WhatsApp Business integration (MF1.3.33's WhatsApp treatment).
- **Strengths:** Particularly strong in markets/regions where WhatsApp is the dominant conversational commerce channel.
- **Weaknesses:** Requires WhatsApp Business API/integration maturity; regionally uneven applicability.
- **Typical Performance:** Strong in WhatsApp-dominant markets; not applicable in markets where the channel sees limited business use.
- **Ideal Objectives:** Engagement, Leads, Sales (conversational commerce).
- **Ideal Funnel Stage:** Mid-to-bottom funnel.
- **Best Industries:** Retail and services in WhatsApp-dominant regions (e.g., parts of Latin America, India, Southeast Asia).
- **Risks:** Same messaging-policy compliance considerations as Messenger Audience (MF5.2.11).
- **Optimization Rules:** Prioritize this Audience type specifically in markets with high WhatsApp business-messaging penetration rather than applying it uniformly globally.

### MF5.2.13 Lead Audience

- **Purpose:** Retarget users who have submitted a Lead (MF1.3.32) via an Instant Form (MF1.3.34) or other Lead-capture mechanism.
- **Construction:** A Custom Audience built from a Lead Event rule, often segmented by downstream qualification status if CRM-fed data is integrated (MF1.5.4's Professional Notes).
- **Strengths:** Captures a demonstrated-interest population appropriate for nurture sequences or Lookalike seeding (MF5.5.1).
- **Weaknesses:** Without CRM-fed quality segmentation, a Lead Audience blends qualified and unqualified submitters indiscriminately.
- **Typical Performance:** Strong as a Lookalike seed source (MF5.5.2) when segmented by genuine qualification; moderate as a direct retargeting pool otherwise.
- **Ideal Objectives:** Leads (nurture retargeting), Sales (Lead-to-Purchase nurture).
- **Ideal Funnel Stage:** Mid funnel.
- **Best Industries:** B2B, real estate, financial services, high-consideration-purchase categories.
- **Risks:** Using an unsegmented Lead Audience as a Lookalike seed, propagating low-quality-lead patterns into new prospecting (mirroring MF1.3.32's Professional Notes).
- **Optimization Rules:** Segment by CRM-fed qualification status wherever available before using as either a retargeting pool or a Lookalike seed.

### MF5.2.14 Customer List Audience

- **Purpose:** Target or exclude a Business's own known customers/contacts, uploaded directly rather than derived from tracked on-platform/website behavior.
- **Construction:** A Custom Audience built by uploading a hashed customer list (email, phone, or other identifying data) for matching against Meta's user base.
- **Strengths:** The highest-certainty first-party data source available — no behavioral inference required, since the underlying relationship (customer, subscriber) is already directly known.
- **Weaknesses:** Match rate depends on data quality/completeness and the overlap between the Business's contact data and Meta's own user identity data.
- **Typical Performance:** Very strong for exclusion use cases (removing existing customers from prospecting) and for Lookalike seeding from high-value-customer segments.
- **Ideal Objectives:** Sales (exclusion from prospecting, retention/upsell targeting), Leads (existing-contact nurture).
- **Ideal Funnel Stage:** Bottom of funnel (existing customers) or as a Lookalike seed for top-of-funnel prospecting.
- **Best Industries:** Universal, particularly valuable for Businesses with a mature CRM.
- **Risks:** The most privacy/compliance-sensitive Audience type in this catalog, given direct PII upload — subject to the full Privacy Compliance and GDPR treatment at MF5.18.6–7.
- **Optimization Rules:** Segment by customer value/tier before Lookalike seeding (MF5.5.2) rather than uploading an undifferentiated full customer list.

### MF5.2.15 Offline Audience

- **Purpose:** Target or exclude users based on offline business outcomes (in-store purchases, phone-closed sales, CRM-recorded qualified leads), extending MF1.6.14's Offline Events to Audience construction.
- **Construction:** A Custom Audience built from uploaded Offline Event data, matched back to Meta user identities via the same mechanism used for Offline Event Attribution.
- **Strengths:** Captures true business outcomes invisible to any on-platform/website tracking, closing the loop for Businesses with significant offline transaction volume.
- **Weaknesses:** Requires a mature offline-data-capture and upload process; typically lower match rate and higher latency than real-time digital Events.
- **Typical Performance:** Strong for Lookalike seeding from genuinely high-value, offline-verified customers; valuable for exclusion purposes.
- **Ideal Objectives:** Sales (Lookalike seeding, exclusion), Leads (offline-qualified nurture).
- **Ideal Funnel Stage:** Bottom of funnel (as a source), top of funnel (as a Lookalike seed for new prospecting).
- **Best Industries:** Retail with physical locations, automotive, real estate, any business with significant offline transaction volume.
- **Risks:** Upload latency causing stale or incomplete Attribution-matching windows (mirroring MF1.6.14's Common Mistakes).
- **Optimization Rules:** Automate Offline Event upload cadence as tightly as the Business's operational systems allow, minimizing the latency gap.

### MF5.2.16 Catalog Audience

- **Purpose:** Target or retarget users based on their interaction with specific Catalog (MF1.3.35) items, powering Catalog Sales/Dynamic Creative (MF3.5.13, MF4.2.10).
- **Construction:** A Custom Audience built from Catalog-linked Event rules (viewed a product, added a product to cart) scoped to a specific Product Set (MF1.3.36).
- **Strengths:** Enables highly specific, product-level retargeting at scale without manually building a separate Audience per product.
- **Weaknesses:** Entirely dependent on Catalog data quality and Pixel/App-Catalog integration completeness (MF1.3.35's Common Mistakes).
- **Typical Performance:** Strong for e-commerce retargeting specifically, particularly for cart-abandonment recovery use cases.
- **Ideal Objectives:** Sales (Catalog Sales sub-type).
- **Ideal Funnel Stage:** Mid-to-bottom funnel.
- **Best Industries:** E-commerce, multi-SKU retail.
- **Risks:** Stale Catalog data producing irrelevant or unavailable-item retargeting (MF1.3.35's Common Mistakes, MF4.2.10's Common Mistakes).
- **Optimization Rules:** Segment Catalog Audiences by Product Set category/margin tier to allow differentiated Bid Strategy (MF2.6) treatment across product economics.

### MF5.2.17 App Audience

- **Purpose:** Target or retarget users based on App-side behavior (installed, opened, completed a specific in-app Event), extending MF1.5.5's App Promotion Objective to Audience construction.
- **Construction:** A Custom Audience built from App SDK-tracked Event rules (App Install, specific in-app Event, App Event value threshold).
- **Strengths:** Enables retargeting of an existing app user base for re-engagement or upsell, and exclusion of already-installed users from App Install prospecting.
- **Weaknesses:** Subject to the same platform-level measurement-completeness constraints (iOS App Tracking Transparency) as App Promotion Optimization generally (MF1.5.5).
- **Typical Performance:** Strong for re-engagement campaigns targeting lapsed users; strong as an exclusion list preventing wasted Install-objective spend on existing users.
- **Ideal Objectives:** App Promotion (re-engagement, App Events sub-type retargeting).
- **Ideal Funnel Stage:** Mid-to-bottom funnel (existing users), or as an exclusion for top-of-funnel Install prospecting.
- **Best Industries:** Mobile games, subscription apps, e-commerce apps.
- **Risks:** Measurement-completeness gaps understating true App Audience size/behavior, particularly on iOS.
- **Optimization Rules:** Always apply an App Audience as an exclusion on App Install-objective Ad Sets to avoid wasted spend re-acquiring existing users.

### MF5.2.18 Audience Type Selection Summary Table

| Audience Type | Best Fit Funnel Stage | Infrastructure Requirement |
|---|---|---|
| Core | Top (cold prospecting) | None |
| Saved | Same as underlying Core config | None |
| Custom (general) | Mid-Bottom | Varies by source |
| Lookalike | Top-Mid (prospecting) | A Custom Audience seed |
| Advantage+ | All | None (Signal optional) |
| Engaged | Upper-Mid | Page/Instagram/on-platform presence |
| Website | Mid-Bottom | Pixel/Conversions API |
| Video | Upper-Mid | Video Creative history |
| Page | Upper | Facebook Page |
| Instagram | Upper-Mid | Instagram Account |
| Messenger | Mid-Bottom | Messenger presence |
| WhatsApp | Mid-Bottom | WhatsApp Business integration |
| Lead | Mid | Lead-capture mechanism |
| Customer List | Bottom (source) / Top (as Lookalike seed) | CRM/contact data |
| Offline | Bottom (source) / Top (as Lookalike seed) | Offline data capture process |
| Catalog | Mid-Bottom | Catalog + Pixel/App integration |
| App | Mid-Bottom (existing users) / exclusion for Top | App SDK integration |

---

## MF5.3 Core Audience Framework

Every Core Audience (MF5.2.1/MF1.8.1) targeting option, each analyzed for: Definition, Configuration Guidance, Common Mistake.

### MF5.3.1 Age

- **Definition:** A numeric age-range criterion bounding eligible users.
- **Configuration Guidance:** Set only as narrow as the product/service genuinely requires (e.g., legal age minimums for regulated categories); avoid narrowing purely on assumption without data support.
- **Common Mistake:** Narrowing Age based on unvalidated stereotypes about who "should" want a product, excluding a genuinely receptive but unexpected segment.

### MF5.3.2 Gender

- **Definition:** A criterion restricting eligibility to a specific gender or leaving it unrestricted (all genders).
- **Configuration Guidance:** Restrict only where the product/service is genuinely gender-specific; default to unrestricted otherwise and let Optimization Goal (MF3.5) and Creative (MF4) do the relevance work.
- **Common Mistake:** Gender-restricting a genuinely gender-neutral product based on assumed-but-unvalidated demographic skew.

### MF5.3.3 Language

- **Definition:** A criterion targeting users based on their platform-language setting.
- **Configuration Guidance:** Match Language targeting to the actual Creative Copy language (MF4.3) — a Language-mismatched Creative wastes spend on users who cannot understand the message.
- **Common Mistake:** Targeting a broad geography without a corresponding Language restriction, delivering a single-language Creative to a linguistically diverse population.

### MF5.3.4 Location

- **Definition:** The overarching geographic-eligibility criterion, specialized into Radius, Country, Region, City, and Postal Code (MF5.3.5–9).
- **Configuration Guidance:** Select the Location granularity matching the Business's actual service area — a national e-commerce brand needs Country-level targeting; a local service business needs Radius/City-level targeting.
- **Common Mistake:** Applying an unnecessarily broad Location for a genuinely local business, wasting spend on geographically unreachable prospects.

### MF5.3.5 Radius

- **Definition:** A circular geographic boundary around a specific point (typically a business location), defined by a distance value.
- **Configuration Guidance:** Size the Radius against genuine service-area reach (delivery range, typical customer travel distance) rather than an arbitrary default.
- **Common Mistake:** Setting a Radius too small to support Learning Phase exit (MF3.10.2) for a low-volume Optimization Event, or too large to be genuinely serviceable by the business.

### MF5.3.6 Country

- **Definition:** A criterion restricting eligibility to one or more entire countries.
- **Configuration Guidance:** Appropriate for national or multi-national e-commerce/digital businesses with no physical service-area constraint.
- **Common Mistake:** Targeting a Country broadly when the Business's actual fulfillment/service capability is limited to a specific region within it.

### MF5.3.7 Region

- **Definition:** A criterion restricting eligibility to a sub-national administrative area (state/province).
- **Configuration Guidance:** Appropriate when service capability, regulatory requirements, or regional messaging relevance vary meaningfully at the Region level.
- **Common Mistake:** Fragmenting Region-level targeting into many small Ad Sets when a single, broader configuration (with Campaign Budget, MF1.3.22, dynamically allocating) would perform more efficiently.

### MF5.3.8 City

- **Definition:** A criterion restricting eligibility to one or more named cities.
- **Configuration Guidance:** Appropriate for local businesses with a single or few specific service locations.
- **Common Mistake:** Using City-level targeting when a Radius around the actual business address would more precisely match true service reach, since city boundaries rarely align exactly with practical service areas.

### MF5.3.9 Postal Code

- **Definition:** A criterion restricting eligibility to one or more specific postal/zip code areas.
- **Configuration Guidance:** Appropriate for the most precise local targeting need, such as a business serving only specific neighborhoods.
- **Common Mistake:** Over-fragmenting into many individual Postal Code Ad Sets rather than grouping genuinely similar areas, unnecessarily thinning Learning Phase data per Ad Set.

### MF5.3.10 Detailed Targeting

- **Definition:** The overarching category of non-geographic, non-demographic targeting criteria — specialized into Interests, Behaviors, Demographics, and their own sub-categories (MF5.3.11–17).
- **Configuration Guidance:** Use as a Signal-quality input (especially under Advantage+ Audience, MF5.6) rather than a rigid, heavily-layered restriction.
- **Common Mistake:** Treating every available Detailed Targeting field as mandatory to fill in, rather than selecting only genuinely relevant criteria.

### MF5.3.11 Interests

- **Definition:** Criteria targeting users based on inferred interests (pages liked, content engaged with) categorized by Meta's system.
- **Configuration Guidance:** Select Interests genuinely aligned with the product category; avoid overly broad or tangentially-related Interest categories that dilute relevance.
- **Common Mistake:** Layering many loosely-related Interests together (an "OR" broadening that can paradoxically still narrow effective reach quality) without testing their individual contribution.

### MF5.3.12 Behaviors

- **Definition:** Criteria targeting users based on inferred purchase behaviors, device usage, or other behavioral classifications.
- **Configuration Guidance:** Use Behaviors that are genuinely predictive for the specific Objective (e.g., a purchase-behavior classification for Sales Objectives) rather than a loosely related classification.
- **Common Mistake:** Assuming Behavior classifications are as precise as first-party Custom Audience data (MF5.2.3) — Behaviors are inferred, not directly observed, and should be weighted accordingly in targeting-confidence decisions.

### MF5.3.13 Demographics

- **Definition:** Criteria targeting users based on inferred life-stage or socioeconomic classifications distinct from basic Age/Gender (MF5.3.1–2) — encompassing Life Events, Education, Job Titles, and Income (MF5.3.14–17).
- **Configuration Guidance:** Use Demographics sparingly and only where genuinely predictive, since these are among the more loosely-inferred targeting signals available.
- **Common Mistake:** Combining multiple Demographic criteria simultaneously (education AND job title AND income), narrowing the eligible pool far more aggressively than each criterion's individual inference confidence would justify.

### MF5.3.14 Life Events

- **Definition:** Criteria targeting users based on inferred recent or upcoming life milestones (recently moved, newly engaged, new parent).
- **Configuration Guidance:** Highly effective for categories with genuine life-event-triggered demand (moving services, wedding/baby products).
- **Common Mistake:** Applying Life Events targeting to a category with no genuine life-event connection, adding narrowing without relevance benefit.

### MF5.3.15 Education

- **Definition:** Criteria targeting users based on inferred education level or field of study.
- **Configuration Guidance:** Relevant for B2B, further-education, or professional-credential-driven categories specifically.
- **Common Mistake:** Using Education criteria as a loose proxy for income or professional status, when a more direct Behavior or Job Title criterion would better match actual intent.

### MF5.3.16 Job Titles

- **Definition:** Criteria targeting users based on self-reported or inferred professional role.
- **Configuration Guidance:** A primary targeting lever for B2B categories, most effective when combined with genuine relevance to the actual buying decision-maker role.
- **Common Mistake:** Targeting a Job Title associated with a role that influences but does not actually hold purchasing authority for the advertised product/service.

### MF5.3.17 Income

- **Definition:** Criteria targeting users based on inferred household income bracket, available in specific markets subject to platform/regulatory availability.
- **Configuration Guidance:** Relevant for genuinely price-tier-differentiated categories (luxury goods, premium financial products).
- **Common Mistake:** Applying Income narrowing to a broadly-accessible product where price sensitivity does not genuinely correlate with the available income brackets as inferred by the platform.

### MF5.3.18 Device

- **Definition:** A criterion restricting eligibility to users on specific device types (mobile, desktop, tablet).
- **Configuration Guidance:** Restrict only when the destination experience is genuinely device-specific (e.g., a mobile-only app install flow); default to unrestricted otherwise.
- **Common Mistake:** Restricting to Desktop for a destination that is, in practice, mobile-optimized and mobile-dominant in actual traffic patterns, needlessly shrinking the eligible pool.

### MF5.3.19 OS (Operating System)

- **Definition:** A criterion restricting eligibility to users on a specific mobile operating system (iOS, Android).
- **Configuration Guidance:** Essential for App Promotion Objectives (MF1.5.5) where the advertised app is platform-specific; irrelevant for most other Objectives.
- **Common Mistake:** Applying OS restriction outside an App Promotion context where it serves no genuine targeting purpose.

### MF5.3.20 Connection

- **Definition:** A criterion targeting or excluding users based on their existing connection to the Business's Page/App (e.g., "people who like this Page" or its exclusion inverse).
- **Configuration Guidance:** Use the exclusion form to prevent showing acquisition-oriented Creative to already-connected users; use the inclusion form for Engagement-objective Ad Sets specifically targeting the existing fan base.
- **Common Mistake:** Failing to apply a Connection exclusion on a prospecting-focused Ad Set, wasting spend showing "discover us" messaging to users who already follow the Page.

### MF5.3.21 Expansion

- **Definition:** A Core Audience-level setting allowing Meta's system to serve beyond the specified Detailed Targeting criteria when it predicts a likely-better-performing impression outside the strict criteria.
- **Configuration Guidance:** Generally beneficial to leave enabled, consistent with this Framework's broad default-toward-breadth guidance (MF5.2.1); disable only with a specific, tested reason for strict criteria adherence.
- **Common Mistake:** Disabling Expansion by default out of an assumption that strict criteria adherence is inherently safer, forfeiting system-identified efficient impressions without a genuine countervailing reason.

### MF5.3.22 Layering

- **Definition:** Combining multiple Detailed Targeting criteria simultaneously (e.g., a specific Interest AND a specific Behavior) to narrow the eligible population.
- **Configuration Guidance:** Layer only when each additional criterion is independently justified by a genuine relevance hypothesis, testing the population-size impact of each added layer before finalizing.
- **Common Mistake:** Layering criteria until the eligible population is too small to support Learning Phase exit (MF3.10.2), the Audience-layer parallel to MF3.3.7's Ad-Set-configuration-level Audience Layering guidance.

### MF5.3.23 Narrowing

- **Definition:** The general practice of reducing Audience size via any combination of the criteria above, as a strategic choice distinct from the specific mechanism used.
- **Configuration Guidance:** Narrow deliberately and incrementally, validating at each step that Learning Phase feasibility (MF3.10.5's Required Events) is preserved.
- **Common Mistake:** Narrowing based on an unvalidated assumption about who the "ideal customer" is, rather than data-validated criteria — the single most common root cause of the Audience Too Small diagnostic (MF3.12.1/MF5.13.1).

### MF5.3.24 Exclusions (Core-Audience-Level)

- See MF3.3.8/MF1.8.6 for the base definition. **Configuration Guidance:** Apply Exclusions deliberately for Connection (MF5.3.20), existing-customer Custom Audiences (MF5.2.14), or known Audience Overlap sources (MF5.10) — never as an undifferentiated blanket restriction. **Common Mistake:** Over-applying Exclusions until the remaining eligible pool is too small to support efficient Delivery (mirroring MF3.3.8's Common Mistakes at this Framework's own depth).

### MF5.3.25 Core Audience Configuration Summary

The overarching Core Audience discipline this section establishes: default toward breadth (Expansion enabled, minimal Layering) unless a specific, validated relevance hypothesis justifies narrowing any individual criterion, and validate every narrowing decision against Learning Phase feasibility (MF3.10.5) before finalizing — a single, consistent principle threading through all 24 targeting options above.

---

## MF5.4 Custom Audience Framework

Every Custom Audience (MF1.8.2/MF5.2.3) data source, each analyzed for: Requirements, Construction, Use Cases, Retention Window, Refresh Strategy, Risks. Where a source directly corresponds to an Audience Type already analyzed at MF5.2, this section adds the technical data-source depth (Requirements, Retention Window, Refresh Strategy) that MF5.2's strategic-level treatment did not cover, citing forward to avoid duplicating Purpose/Strengths/Weaknesses content.

### MF5.4.1 Website

- **Requirements:** A Pixel (MF1.3.6) or Conversions API (MF5.4.3) integration firing standard or custom Events (MF1.6).
- **Construction:** Define an Event rule (e.g., "View Content in last 30 days") scoped optionally by URL parameters or Event value.
- **Use Cases:** See MF5.2.7 (Website Audience) for full strategic treatment.
- **Retention Window:** Configurable up to the platform's maximum lookback (commonly 180 days); shorter windows for high-consideration-cycle categories, longer for infrequent-purchase categories.
- **Refresh Strategy:** Automatically refreshes on a rolling basis as the underlying Event rule re-evaluates continuously — no manual refresh action required, but periodic review of the Event rule's continued relevance is still warranted.
- **Risks:** Pixel-only implementations face growing browser-tracking degradation; Conversions API supplementation is increasingly necessary for retention-window reliability.

### MF5.4.2 Pixel

- **Requirements:** Pixel installation (MF1.3.6) on the Business's website with correct Event firing.
- **Construction:** The underlying tracking mechanism enabling Website (MF5.4.1) and other browser-side Custom Audience construction; not itself a distinct Audience source but the infrastructure enabling one.
- **Use Cases:** Foundational infrastructure for all website-behavior-based Custom Audiences.
- **Retention Window:** Not applicable directly to the Pixel itself; governs the Retention Window of Audiences built from it (MF5.4.1).
- **Refresh Strategy:** Not applicable; the Pixel itself requires only periodic implementation-health verification (correct Event firing, Matching Quality, MF1.6.17), not "refresh" in the Audience-recency sense.
- **Risks:** Implementation drift (an Event silently breaking after a website update) going undetected without periodic Events Manager (MF1.3.10) review.

### MF5.4.3 Conversions API

- **Requirements:** Server-side integration per MF1.3.7, ideally deployed alongside Pixel for Deduplication (MF1.6.16).
- **Construction:** Server-fired Events matched to the same Event schema as Pixel Events, improving completeness where browser-side tracking is degraded.
- **Use Cases:** Supplements or, in privacy-constrained contexts, substitutes for Pixel-only Website Audiences (MF5.4.1), improving Retention Window reliability.
- **Retention Window:** Same configurable range as Pixel-based Audiences, with generally higher actual data completeness within that window.
- **Refresh Strategy:** Same rolling, automatic refresh as Pixel-based Audiences.
- **Risks:** Improper Deduplication configuration (MF1.6.16) causing double-counted Events, inflating apparent Audience size or Event volume inaccurately.

### MF5.4.4 Customer Lists

- **Requirements:** A hashed, properly formatted list of customer identifiers (email, phone, or other supported identifiers) uploaded directly.
- **Construction:** See MF5.2.14 (Customer List Audience) for full strategic treatment; technically constructed via direct file upload or an ongoing CRM-integration feed.
- **Use Cases:** Exclusion from prospecting, retention/upsell targeting, Lookalike seeding from high-value segments.
- **Retention Window:** Persists until manually refreshed or removed — unlike Event-based Custom Audiences, a Customer List does not automatically expire on a rolling window.
- **Refresh Strategy:** Requires a manual or automated re-upload cadence to remain current; a one-time upload becomes progressively stale as the actual customer base evolves.
- **Risks:** The highest privacy/compliance sensitivity in this catalog — full treatment at MF5.18.6–7.

### MF5.4.5 Email

- **Requirements:** A list or ongoing feed of customer email addresses, hashed before upload per platform requirements.
- **Construction:** A specific identifier type within Customer Lists (MF5.4.4), matched against Meta's user base via hashed comparison.
- **Use Cases:** The most commonly available and highest-match-rate Customer List identifier type for most Businesses.
- **Retention Window:** Same as Customer Lists generally (MF5.4.4) — persists until refreshed.
- **Refresh Strategy:** Same manual/automated re-upload requirement as Customer Lists generally.
- **Risks:** Same privacy/compliance sensitivity as Customer Lists generally (MF5.18.6–7), plus email-list quality/deliverability-adjacent data-hygiene concerns.

### MF5.4.6 Phone

- **Requirements:** A list or ongoing feed of customer phone numbers, hashed and formatted (with country code) per platform requirements.
- **Construction:** A specific identifier type within Customer Lists (MF5.4.4).
- **Use Cases:** Particularly valuable for Businesses whose primary customer contact channel is phone-based (Calls-optimized categories, MF3.5.14) rather than email.
- **Retention Window:** Same as Customer Lists generally.
- **Refresh Strategy:** Same manual/automated re-upload requirement as Customer Lists generally.
- **Risks:** Same privacy/compliance sensitivity as Customer Lists generally; formatting inconsistency (missing country codes, inconsistent formatting) can depress match rate specifically for this identifier type.

### MF5.4.7 Lead Forms

- **Requirements:** An Instant Form (MF1.3.34) configuration with sufficient submission volume.
- **Construction:** See MF5.2.13 (Lead Audience) for full strategic treatment; technically constructed from Instant Form submission Events.
- **Use Cases:** Nurture retargeting, Lookalike seeding (ideally CRM-quality-segmented first).
- **Retention Window:** Configurable up to platform maximum, similarly to Website Audiences.
- **Refresh Strategy:** Automatic rolling refresh as new submissions accrue.
- **Risks:** Unsegmented-by-quality use as a Lookalike seed propagating low-quality-lead patterns (MF5.2.13's Risks).

### MF5.4.8 Instagram Engagement

- **Requirements:** A connected Instagram Account (MF1.3.5) with sufficient engagement volume.
- **Construction:** See MF5.2.10 (Instagram Audience) for full strategic treatment.
- **Use Cases:** Engagement/Awareness retargeting, visually-driven Sales retargeting.
- **Retention Window:** Configurable up to platform maximum (commonly 365 days for engagement-based sources).
- **Refresh Strategy:** Automatic rolling refresh.
- **Risks:** Overlap with Facebook Engagement (MF5.4.9) sources for cross-platform-engaged users.

### MF5.4.9 Facebook Engagement

- **Requirements:** A connected Facebook Page (MF1.3.4) with sufficient engagement volume.
- **Construction:** See MF5.2.9 (Page Audience) for full strategic treatment.
- **Use Cases:** Engagement/Awareness retargeting.
- **Retention Window:** Configurable up to platform maximum, similar to Instagram Engagement.
- **Refresh Strategy:** Automatic rolling refresh.
- **Risks:** Overlap with Instagram Engagement (MF5.4.8); risk of overly passive interaction types diluting Audience intent quality (MF5.2.9's Risks).

### MF5.4.10 Messenger

- **Requirements:** An active Messenger presence with conversation volume.
- **Construction:** See MF5.2.11 (Messenger Audience) for full strategic treatment.
- **Use Cases:** Conversational-commerce retargeting, Leads nurture.
- **Retention Window:** Configurable up to platform maximum.
- **Refresh Strategy:** Automatic rolling refresh.
- **Risks:** Messaging-policy compliance considerations (MF5.2.11's Risks).

### MF5.4.11 WhatsApp

- **Requirements:** WhatsApp Business API/integration with conversation volume.
- **Construction:** See MF5.2.12 (WhatsApp Audience) for full strategic treatment.
- **Use Cases:** Conversational-commerce retargeting in WhatsApp-dominant markets.
- **Retention Window:** Configurable up to platform maximum.
- **Refresh Strategy:** Automatic rolling refresh.
- **Risks:** Regionally uneven applicability; same messaging-policy considerations as Messenger.

### MF5.4.12 Video Views

- **Requirements:** Published video Creative (MF4.2.2/4.10) with sufficient view volume.
- **Construction:** See MF5.2.8 (Video Audience) for full strategic treatment.
- **Use Cases:** Engagement/Awareness-to-consideration nurture, Sales retargeting for video-heavy categories.
- **Retention Window:** Configurable up to platform maximum, similar to other engagement-based sources.
- **Refresh Strategy:** Automatic rolling refresh as new views accrue; requires ongoing video Creative publication to sustain volume.
- **Risks:** Shallow-threshold configurations carrying too little genuine intent signal (MF5.2.8's Risks).

### MF5.4.13 Page Activity

- **Requirements:** Same as Facebook Engagement (MF5.4.9); "Page Activity" refers to the specific Event-rule granularity available within that source (visits, specific post interactions, message initiations) rather than a distinct source.
- **Construction:** Segment the Facebook Engagement source (MF5.4.9) by specific interaction type rather than a blanket rule.
- **Use Cases:** Finer-grained Engagement retargeting than an undifferentiated Page Audience.
- **Retention Window:** Same as Facebook Engagement generally.
- **Refresh Strategy:** Same automatic rolling refresh.
- **Risks:** Same as Facebook Engagement generally, mitigated somewhat by the finer segmentation itself (MF5.2.9's Optimization Rules).

### MF5.4.14 Shopping Activity

- **Requirements:** Pixel/Conversions API/App SDK integration tracking Shopping-relevant Events (View Content, Add To Cart, Initiate Checkout, Purchase, MF1.6.2/5–6/8).
- **Construction:** An Event-rule combination specifically scoped to the Shopping funnel sequence, as distinct from a single generic Website Audience (MF5.4.1) rule.
- **Use Cases:** Sales retargeting segmented by specific Shopping funnel stage (cart-abandonment recovery being the most common high-value use case).
- **Retention Window:** Typically shorter than general Website Audiences (7–14 days for cart-abandonment specifically) to match realistic purchase-decision timelines.
- **Refresh Strategy:** Automatic rolling refresh, with the short Retention Window itself functioning as a natural, continuous refresh cycle.
- **Risks:** An overly long Retention Window for cart-abandonment specifically retargeting users well past their realistic purchase-decision window.

### MF5.4.15 Catalog

- **Requirements:** A connected Catalog (MF1.3.35) and Pixel/App-Catalog integration.
- **Construction:** See MF5.2.16 (Catalog Audience) for full strategic treatment.
- **Use Cases:** Product-level retargeting, Catalog Sales (MF3.5.13).
- **Retention Window:** Configurable, commonly matched to the Shopping Activity source's shorter windows (MF5.4.14) for cart-abandonment-style Catalog retargeting.
- **Refresh Strategy:** Automatic rolling refresh, dependent on continued Catalog data freshness (MF1.3.35's Common Mistakes).
- **Risks:** Stale Catalog data producing irrelevant retargeting (MF5.2.16's Risks).

### MF5.4.16 Offline Events

- **Requirements:** An offline-data-capture and upload process (POS, CRM) per MF1.6.14.
- **Construction:** See MF5.2.15 (Offline Audience) for full strategic treatment.
- **Use Cases:** Lookalike seeding from verified high-value offline customers, exclusion of existing offline customers from prospecting.
- **Retention Window:** Dependent on upload cadence and the Attribution-matching window (MF1.7.3) applicable to the uploaded Events.
- **Refresh Strategy:** Requires an ongoing, ideally automated, upload cadence — a one-time upload becomes stale quickly given offline data's typically slower capture-to-upload latency.
- **Risks:** Upload latency causing stale or incomplete matching (MF5.2.15's Risks).

### MF5.4.17 App Activity

- **Requirements:** App SDK integration (MF1.3.7-adjacent, mobile measurement) tracking installs and in-app Events.
- **Construction:** See MF5.2.17 (App Audience) for full strategic treatment.
- **Use Cases:** Re-engagement of existing app users, exclusion from Install-objective prospecting.
- **Retention Window:** Configurable up to platform maximum, subject to platform measurement-completeness constraints (particularly iOS).
- **Refresh Strategy:** Automatic rolling refresh as new App Events accrue.
- **Risks:** Measurement-completeness gaps understating true Audience size/behavior (MF5.2.17's Risks).

### MF5.4.18 Custom Audience Source Selection Rule

Select the Custom Audience source matching the Business's most mature, highest-quality available data infrastructure first (typically Website/Shopping Activity for e-commerce, Customer Lists for CRM-mature Businesses, Offline Events where offline transaction volume is significant) — layering additional, lower-maturity sources only where they add genuinely incremental, non-redundant coverage rather than defaulting to enabling every available source indiscriminately.

---

## MF5.5 Lookalike Framework

Extends MF1.8.3/MF5.2.4 (Lookalike Audience) with the complete construction and scaling methodology.

### MF5.5.1 Seed Audiences

- **Definition:** The source Custom Audience (MF1.8.2) a Lookalike Audience is generated from — the population Meta's system analyzes to infer shared characteristics for prospecting.
- **Guidance:** Select a Seed representing the Business's genuinely highest-value users (e.g., repeat purchasers, high-AOV customers, CRM-qualified leads) rather than the broadest available pool (e.g., all website visitors).
- **Common Mistake:** Seeding from a low-specificity source (all Page engagers) when a more specific, higher-value source (Purchasers, MF1.6.8) is available, producing a lower-quality Lookalike than the data would otherwise support.

### MF5.5.2 Seed Quality

- **Definition:** The degree to which a Seed Audience genuinely represents the characteristics the Business wants to find more of, determined by both its specificity (how well-defined the underlying behavior is) and its volume (sufficient size for the system to identify reliable shared patterns).
- **Guidance:** Prioritize Seed Quality over Seed size alone — a smaller, highly-specific high-value-customer Seed generally produces a better Lookalike than a larger but generic Seed.
- **Common Mistake:** Using an unsegmented Customer List (MF5.2.14) or Lead Audience (MF5.2.13) as a Seed without first filtering for genuine quality/value, propagating low-value patterns into new prospecting (mirroring MF5.2.13's Risks).

### MF5.5.3 Similarity

- **Definition:** The percentage parameter controlling how closely a Lookalike Audience must resemble its Seed, with lower percentages indicating tighter similarity and smaller resulting Audience size.
- **Guidance:** Start narrow (1%, MF5.5.5) for new Lookalikes, widening only once Learning Limited (MF3.10.2) or Budget Saturation (MF2.10.8) indicates the current population is too small to support the Ad Set's Budget/Bid Strategy.
- **Common Mistake:** Selecting a wide similarity percentage from the outset without first validating the tighter tier's performance, forfeiting the opportunity to learn the true similarity-vs-quality tradeoff for the specific Seed.

### MF5.5.4 Expansion (Lookalike-Specific)

- **Definition:** The practice of widening a Lookalike's Similarity percentage over time as a Scaling lever, extending MF3.13.4's Ad-Set-layer Lookalike Expansion treatment with this Framework's own strategic depth.
- **Guidance:** Expand incrementally (one tier at a time, e.g., 1% → 2%, not 1% → 10%) to preserve the ability to attribute any resulting performance change to the specific expansion step.
- **Common Mistake:** Jumping multiple similarity tiers in one step, both constituting an outsized significant edit (MF3.10.4) and confounding the ability to identify the point at which quality genuinely degrades.

### MF5.5.5 1% Lookalike

- **Characteristics:** The tightest, most similar-to-Seed tier available, producing the smallest resulting Audience.
- **Guidance:** The recommended starting tier for any new Lookalike, prioritizing quality/relevance over reach.
- **Best Fit:** Early-stage testing of a new Seed source; Sales/Leads Objectives prioritizing efficiency over volume.

### MF5.5.6 2% Lookalike

- **Characteristics:** A moderately wider tier than 1%, trading a small amount of similarity precision for increased Audience size.
- **Guidance:** The typical first Expansion step once a 1% Lookalike shows Learning Limited or Budget Saturation symptoms.
- **Best Fit:** Scaling a validated 1% Lookalike's success incrementally.

### MF5.5.7 3% Lookalike

- **Characteristics:** A further-widened tier, continuing the same similarity-vs-reach tradeoff progression.
- **Guidance:** Apply only after validating that the 2% tier's performance remains acceptable relative to the 1% baseline.
- **Best Fit:** Continued incremental Scaling for Businesses with strong Seed Quality (MF5.5.2) able to sustain quality at wider tiers.

### MF5.5.8 5% Lookalike

- **Characteristics:** A meaningfully broader tier, generally showing a more noticeable quality-vs-1% gap than the 2–3% steps.
- **Guidance:** Monitor Primary KPI (MF3.11.1) degradation closely at this tier specifically, as this is often the point where the similarity-vs-reach tradeoff becomes clearly visible.
- **Best Fit:** Businesses with very strong, high-volume Seed Quality where broader reach is a genuine current priority.

### MF5.5.9 10% Lookalike

- **Characteristics:** The broadest standard tier, approaching Core Audience (MF5.2.1)-like breadth while retaining some Seed-informed relevance.
- **Guidance:** Reserve for Businesses that have validated acceptable performance at every intermediate tier, or for genuinely volume-prioritized Objectives (Awareness).
- **Best Fit:** Maximum-reach prospecting once tighter tiers are fully saturated (MF5.11) and further Horizontal Scaling (MF3.13.1) capacity is needed.

### MF5.5.10 Broad Lookalikes

- **Definition:** A general term for Lookalikes at the wider end of the tier spectrum (5–10%, MF5.5.8–9), used here to distinguish the broad-tier strategic pattern from any single specific percentage.
- **Guidance:** Broad Lookalikes function as a middle ground between tight Lookalike precision and full Advantage+ Audience (MF5.6) system-driven breadth — consider Advantage+ Audience directly as an alternative once Lookalike tiers reach this breadth, since the marginal precision benefit over full AI-driven expansion narrows at wider tiers.
- **Common Mistake:** Defaulting straight to a Broad Lookalike without validating tighter tiers first, skipping the incremental learning process this Framework's Similarity guidance (MF5.5.3) establishes.

### MF5.5.11 Multiple Lookalikes

- **Definition:** Running several Lookalike Audiences simultaneously (e.g., a 1% and a 3% from the same or different Seeds) across sibling Ad Sets.
- **Guidance:** Ensure adequate differentiation between simultaneous Lookalikes (different Seeds, or non-adjacent Similarity tiers) to avoid Audience Overlap (MF5.10).
- **Common Mistake:** Running adjacent tiers (e.g., 1% and 2%) from the identical Seed simultaneously, producing substantial overlap given the inherently nested nature of Lookalike tiers (a 2% Lookalike generally contains most of the corresponding 1% population).

### MF5.5.12 Value-Based Lookalikes

- **Definition:** A Lookalike generated from a Seed weighted by customer value (e.g., lifetime value or Purchase value) rather than simple membership, allowing the system to prioritize resemblance to the highest-value portion of the Seed specifically.
- **Guidance:** Use whenever a value-differentiated Seed is available (e.g., a Customer List with attached purchase-value data) — Value-Based Lookalikes generally outperform simple membership-based ones for Sales Objectives specifically.
- **Common Mistake:** Using a simple membership-based Lookalike when value data is available but unused, forfeiting a meaningful quality improvement at no additional Seed-collection cost.

### MF5.5.13 Country Dependency

- **Definition:** The Lookalike generation process's dependency on the target country/region specified at creation — a Lookalike is generated independently per country, and Seed data from one country does not directly inform Lookalike quality in another.
- **Guidance:** For multi-country expansion, build a separate Lookalike per target country rather than assuming a single Lookalike generalizes across borders, particularly where the Seed itself is concentrated in one country.
- **Common Mistake:** Expecting a Lookalike built from a single-country Seed to perform equivalently well when applied to prospecting in an entirely different country/market.

### MF5.5.14 Scaling Strategy (Lookalike-Specific)

Extends MF3.13.4 (Lookalike Expansion) and MF5.5.4 (Expansion) into a complete strategy: (1) validate a 1% Lookalike from the highest-quality available Seed; (2) expand incrementally through 2–3% as Budget Saturation (MF2.10.8) demands; (3) introduce Multiple Lookalikes (MF5.5.11) from differentiated Seeds before jumping to Broad tiers; (4) consider Advantage+ Audience (MF5.6) as the natural next step once Broad Lookalike tiers (5–10%) are reached, per MF5.5.10's guidance.

### MF5.5.15 Limitations

- Entirely dependent on Seed Quality (MF5.5.2) — no Similarity tier can compensate for a fundamentally low-quality or insufficiently-sized Seed.
- Country Dependency (MF5.5.13) requires per-market Seed and Lookalike construction, adding operational overhead for multi-country Businesses.
- A Lookalike's underlying Seed can become stale over time (mirroring MF3.3.2's Common Mistakes), requiring periodic Seed refresh (MF5.4's Refresh Strategy guidance) to maintain Lookalike quality.
- At Broad tiers (5–10%), the precision advantage over Advantage+ Audience's fully system-driven approach narrows, raising the question of whether continued manual Lookalike management is still the optimal strategy versus a full AI-delegation approach (MF5.6).

---

## MF5.6 Advantage+ Audience Framework

Extends MF1.8.4/MF5.2.5 (Advantage+ Audience) with the complete strategic treatment.

### MF5.6.1 Signals (cross-reference)

Fully defined at MF3.3.6. Framework-level significance: Signal quality is the single highest-leverage input a Business retains under Advantage+ Audience — see MF5.6.8's Strengths/Weaknesses balance below.

### MF5.6.2 AI Expansion

- **Definition:** The mechanism by which Meta's system extends Delivery beyond any supplied Signal's literal boundaries when it predicts a likely-better-performing impression exists outside them, the Advantage+ Audience-specific instance of the general Expansion concept (MF5.3.21).
- **Role:** The core differentiator between Advantage+ Audience and a Lookalike Audience (MF5.5) — Lookalike similarity tiers impose a bounded resemblance constraint, while AI Expansion under Advantage+ Audience has no fixed percentage ceiling.
- **Guidance:** Trust AI Expansion's breadth for prospecting-oriented Ad Sets; monitor Placement/Audience Breakdown (MF1.3.39) periodically to confirm the expanded population remains genuinely relevant, not merely broad.

### MF5.6.3 Automation

- **Definition:** The overarching delegation of population-definition decisions to Meta's system, of which AI Expansion (MF5.6.2) and Signal-weighted inference are the specific mechanisms.
- **Role:** Represents the philosophical endpoint of the AI-delegation spectrum this Framework has tracked from Core Audience's (MF5.2.1) full manual control through Lookalike's (MF5.5) partial delegation to this fully-automated approach.
- **Guidance:** Appropriate as the default starting point for most new Ad Sets per current platform guidance (MF3.3.4); manual Core Audience configuration should be the deliberate exception requiring specific justification.

### MF5.6.4 Optimization Logic

- **Definition:** The way Advantage+ Audience's population inference interacts with the Ad Set's configured Optimization Goal (MF3.5) — the system does not merely find "similar" users generically, but specifically users predicted to be likely to complete the configured Optimization Event.
- **Role:** Ties Audience-layer AI delegation directly to Optimization-layer configuration, meaning the same Advantage+ Audience settings can produce meaningfully different actual populations depending on which Optimization Event is configured.
- **Guidance:** Re-evaluate Advantage+ Audience Signal relevance whenever the Optimization Event changes (MF3.13.7's Optimization Changes), since a Signal well-suited to one Optimization Event may be less relevant to another.

### MF5.6.5 Learning Behavior

- **Definition:** How Advantage+ Audience-configured Ad Sets interact with Learning Phase (MF1.3.19/MF3.10) — generally exhibiting faster, more reliable Learning Phase exit than narrowly-configured Core Audience or tight Lookalike Ad Sets, given the larger effective eligible population.
- **Role:** A primary reason this Framework and MF3.3.4 both recommend Advantage+ Audience as the default — its Learning Behavior directly mitigates the Learning Limited risk (MF3.10.2) that narrower configurations are more prone to.
- **Guidance:** For genuinely low-volume Optimization Events (e.g., a high-AOV Purchase, MF3.5.11), Advantage+ Audience's Learning Behavior advantage is especially valuable, since it maximizes the eligible population available to accumulate the needed Event volume.

### MF5.6.6 Business Cases

Prospecting for new customers with no established Lookalike Seed yet; scaling beyond a Broad Lookalike tier's (MF5.5.10) diminishing precision advantage; any Ad Set where Learning Limited risk (MF3.10.2) is a primary concern given a low-volume Optimization Event; Businesses newly adopting Meta Ads without yet having built first-party Custom Audience infrastructure (MF5.4).

### MF5.6.7 Strengths

Current platform-recommended default with the strongest documented Learning Phase and scaling characteristics (MF5.6.5); reduces manual targeting-configuration burden; benefits directly from continuous platform-side algorithmic improvement without requiring the Business to manually re-tune criteria.

### MF5.6.8 Weaknesses

Reduced granular brand-safety/manual control relative to Core Audience (MF5.2.1); effectiveness is bounded by Signal quality (MF5.6.1/MF3.3.6's Common Mistakes) — a weak or absent Signal yields weaker inference than a well-chosen Signal would; less transparent/explainable population composition than a manually-defined Core Audience, which can complicate Diagnostics (MF5.13) root-cause isolation in some cases.

### MF5.6.9 Restrictions

Advantage+ Audience's AI Expansion (MF5.6.2) is not available or is more constrained in certain regulated categories (housing, employment, credit) subject to platform special-category advertising policies, where narrower, non-discriminatory targeting requirements apply regardless of the Business's preferred Automation level; Businesses in these categories must consult current platform policy for applicable Core Audience-level restrictions that override the general default-toward-Advantage+-Audience guidance.

### MF5.6.10 Governance

Document the Signal (MF5.6.1) supplied and its rationale for every Advantage+ Audience-configured Ad Set (extending MF3.15.2's Ad-Set-layer Documentation requirement); verify special-category Restrictions (MF5.6.9) applicability at Approval Workflow (MF3.15.4/MF5.18.4) before launch; periodically audit Signal relevance as Optimization Events or Business focus evolve (MF5.18.10's Audit Standards).

---

## MF5.7 Audience Psychology Framework

Where MF4.4 catalogued the psychological principles a Creative deploys, this section catalogues the corresponding behavioral/psychological *states* an Audience segment may be in — the receiving end of those Creative principles. Each is documented with: Definition, Behavioral Signals, Audience/Targeting Implication, Relationship to Creative Psychology.

### MF5.7.1 Awareness (Audience State)

- **Definition:** The state of an Audience segment having no or minimal prior exposure to the Business/product.
- **Behavioral Signals:** No engagement history, no Custom Audience membership, cold Core/Advantage+ Audience population.
- **Audience/Targeting Implication:** Appropriate for Awareness-objective (MF1.5.1) targeting via Core, Lookalike, or Advantage+ Audience — no retargeting-based Custom Audience is relevant here by definition.
- **Relationship to Creative Psychology:** Pairs with Attention/Curiosity/Pattern Interrupt Creative principles (MF4.4.1–2/13), since the Audience has no prior context to draw on.

### MF5.7.2 Interest

- **Definition:** The state of an Audience segment having demonstrated initial engagement (a click, a view, a Page interaction) without yet showing deeper purchase-consideration signals.
- **Behavioral Signals:** Engaged Audience (MF5.2.6) or shallow Video Audience (MF5.2.8) membership.
- **Audience/Targeting Implication:** Appropriate for Engagement-objective retargeting and as a Custom Audience source for further nurture sequencing or Lookalike seeding once deeper signals accrue.
- **Relationship to Creative Psychology:** Pairs with Curiosity and Educational Hook-adjacent principles (MF4.4.2, MF4.5.10), continuing to build engagement depth.

### MF5.7.3 Intent

- **Definition:** The state of an Audience segment having demonstrated specific, purchase-consideration-relevant behavior (View Content, Add To Wishlist, deep Video watch percentage).
- **Behavioral Signals:** Website Audience (MF5.2.7) scoped to View Content/Add To Wishlist Events (MF1.6.2/4); deep (75%+) Video Audience thresholds.
- **Audience/Targeting Implication:** A strong Lookalike Seed candidate (MF5.5.1) and a primary Sales/Leads retargeting pool.
- **Relationship to Creative Psychology:** Pairs with Offer and Value Proposition-led Creative (MF4.3.8–9), since baseline interest is established and a concrete next step is now appropriate.

### MF5.7.4 Purchase Readiness

- **Definition:** The state of an Audience segment having demonstrated the strongest pre-transaction behavioral signal short of the transaction itself (Add To Cart, Initiate Checkout).
- **Behavioral Signals:** Shopping Activity Custom Audience (MF5.4.14) scoped to Add To Cart/Initiate Checkout.
- **Audience/Targeting Implication:** The highest-priority Sales retargeting pool, warranting the shortest Retention Window (MF5.4.14) and often a dedicated, higher-Bid-Strategy-tolerance Ad Set.
- **Relationship to Creative Psychology:** Pairs with Urgency, Loss Aversion, and Offer Hooks (MF4.4.6–7, MF4.5.9), directly targeting the final decision moment.

### MF5.7.5 Trust (Audience State)

- **Definition:** The degree to which an Audience segment already holds confidence in the Business, independent of purchase-funnel stage — a returning-customer segment carries inherent Trust a cold-prospect segment does not.
- **Behavioral Signals:** Customer List Audience (MF5.2.14) membership; repeat-Purchase Website Audience segments.
- **Audience/Targeting Implication:** High-Trust segments require less Creative-level Trust-building (MF4.3.10–11/18–19) and can be targeted with more direct Offer/CTA-led Creative.
- **Relationship to Creative Psychology:** Inversely related to Creative Trust-element intensity requirements (MF4.4.11) — the more inherent Audience Trust, the less Creative-level Trust-building is needed to convert.

### MF5.7.6 Risk (Audience State)

- **Definition:** The degree of perceived risk (financial, time, reputational) a given transaction represents to a specific Audience segment, which varies by both product category and the segment's own prior experience.
- **Behavioral Signals:** New-prospect segments (MF5.7.1) inherently carry higher perceived Risk than repeat-customer segments (MF5.7.5); higher-price-point Product Set (MF1.3.36) engagement signals higher-Risk consideration.
- **Audience/Targeting Implication:** High-perceived-Risk segments warrant pairing with Guarantee/Trust-heavy Creative (MF4.3.18) and potentially a longer nurture sequence (via Funnel Audience staging, MF5.8) before a direct Sales ask.
- **Relationship to Creative Psychology:** Directly informs how heavily a Creative should lean on Trust/Authority/Social Proof principles (MF4.4.8/10–11) for a given Audience segment.

### MF5.7.7 Motivation

- **Definition:** The underlying driver behind an Audience segment's potential action — which specific Creative Psychology principle (MF4.4) is likely to resonate most, given the segment's known characteristics or behavior.
- **Behavioral Signals:** Inferred from Detailed Targeting criteria (MF5.3.10–17), Life Events (MF5.3.14), or segment-specific engagement patterns.
- **Audience/Targeting Implication:** Should inform Creative selection per segment (e.g., an Identity-driven Motivation for a lifestyle-brand Audience segment vs. a Reciprocity-driven Motivation for a B2B research-stage segment), not a single universal Creative applied indiscriminately.
- **Relationship to Creative Psychology:** The Audience-side counterpart determining which of MF4.4's 18 principles is most likely to land for a given segment.

### MF5.7.8 Pain (Audience State)

- **Definition:** A specific, identifiable frustration or unmet need a given Audience segment experiences, which the Business's product/service addresses.
- **Behavioral Signals:** Search-behavior-adjacent Detailed Targeting (MF5.3.11), Job Title/Life Event segments known to correlate with the specific Pain point.
- **Audience/Targeting Implication:** Segments with a clearly identifiable Pain point are well-suited to Pain Hook-led Creative (MF4.5.5) and problem-solution Sales/Leads messaging.
- **Relationship to Creative Psychology:** Directly informs Pain Hook and Problem Hook selection (MF4.5.5/14) at the Creative layer.

### MF5.7.9 Identity (Audience State)

- **Definition:** The self-concept or aspirational self-image a given Audience segment holds, which a product/service may align with.
- **Behavioral Signals:** Interest/Behavior Detailed Targeting categories (MF5.3.11–12) associated with a specific lifestyle or self-expression pattern.
- **Audience/Targeting Implication:** Lifestyle/fashion/community-oriented Audience segments are well-suited to Identity-led Creative (MF4.4.15); purely utility-driven segments are not.
- **Relationship to Creative Psychology:** Directly informs Identity and Community principle selection (MF4.4.15–16) at the Creative layer.

### MF5.7.10 Urgency (Audience State)

- **Definition:** The degree to which a given Audience segment faces a genuine time constraint relevant to the Business's Offer (e.g., a segment engaging during a known limited-time event window).
- **Behavioral Signals:** Engagement timing correlated with a genuine promotional or seasonal window (MF3.8.7–8).
- **Audience/Targeting Implication:** Only pair Urgency-led Creative (MF4.4.6) with segments for whom the Urgency claim is genuinely applicable — applying it universally regardless of actual segment-level relevance risks the fabrication/Trust-erosion pattern flagged at MF4.3.16.
- **Relationship to Creative Psychology:** Directly gates whether Urgency Creative principles (MF4.4.6) are honestly applicable to a given segment.

### MF5.7.11 Buying Stage

- **Definition:** The overarching classification of an Audience segment's position within the general purchase-consideration sequence, synthesizing Awareness/Interest/Intent/Purchase Readiness (MF5.7.1–4) into a single funnel-position label.
- **Behavioral Signals:** The specific combination of Custom Audience sources (MF5.4) and Event recency/depth a segment qualifies under.
- **Audience/Targeting Implication:** The primary input to Funnel Audience Framework (MF5.8) staging — every TOF/MOF/BOF designation in that section is fundamentally a Buying Stage classification.
- **Relationship to Creative Psychology:** Determines which Objective (MF1.5) and which Creative funnel-stage treatment (MF4.2's Ideal Objectives fields) is appropriate for the segment.

### MF5.7.12 Decision Stage

- **Definition:** A narrower, more immediate classification than Buying Stage (MF5.7.11), specifically indicating whether a segment is actively comparing options (Comparison-Hook-relevant, MF4.5.12) or has already effectively decided and merely needs a final prompt (Offer/Urgency-Hook-relevant, MF4.5.9/MF4.4.6).
- **Behavioral Signals:** Purchase Readiness-tier (MF5.7.4) Shopping Activity signals specifically, as distinct from earlier Intent-tier signals.
- **Audience/Targeting Implication:** The most precise segment for a final-conversion-push Ad Set, warranting the most direct, lowest-friction CTA (MF4.6.1/9, Shop Now/Get Offer).
- **Relationship to Creative Psychology:** Directly gates the choice between Comparison Hooks (earlier Decision Stage) and Offer/Urgency Hooks (later Decision Stage).

### MF5.7.13 Loyalty

- **Definition:** The state of an existing-customer Audience segment continuing to engage with and purchase from the Business over time, distinct from a first-time-converting segment.
- **Behavioral Signals:** Repeat-Purchase Customer List (MF5.2.14) or Website Audience segments; high-frequency Shopping Activity engagement over an extended period.
- **Audience/Targeting Implication:** The primary target for Retention, Upsell, and Cross-sell Funnel Audience stages (MF5.8.4–6), warranting different Creative and Offer treatment than acquisition-focused segments.
- **Relationship to Creative Psychology:** Pairs with Consistency and Community principles (MF4.4.16/18), reinforcing an already-established relationship rather than establishing a new one.

### MF5.7.14 Advocacy

- **Definition:** The state of a Loyalty-tier (MF5.7.13) Audience segment actively promoting the Business to others (referrals, reviews, Shares of Business Creative, MF4.9.11).
- **Behavioral Signals:** High Share Rate (MF4.9.11) engagement with Business content; participation in a referral program where tracked as a Custom Audience source.
- **Audience/Targeting Implication:** The target for the Referral and Advocacy Funnel Audience stages (MF5.8.9–10); also a strong Lookalike Seed candidate (MF5.5.1) given Advocacy's correlation with genuine high-value customer status.
- **Relationship to Creative Psychology:** Pairs with Social Proof and Community principles (MF4.4.10/16) — an Advocacy-stage segment is itself often the source of the Social Proof used to reach other segments.

### MF5.7.15 Audience Psychology Summary Table

| State | Buying Stage Alignment | Best Fit Creative Psychology (MF4.4) |
|---|---|---|
| Awareness | TOF | Attention, Curiosity, Pattern Interrupt |
| Interest | TOF-MOF | Curiosity, Reciprocity |
| Intent | MOF | Offer, Value Proposition-led |
| Purchase Readiness | BOF | Urgency, Loss Aversion |
| Trust (high) | Any (inherent) | Reduced Trust-building need |
| Risk (high) | Any | Authority, Trust, Guarantee-heavy |
| Pain (identified) | TOF-MOF | Pain/Problem Hooks |
| Identity-aligned | Any | Identity, Community |
| Loyalty | Retention | Consistency, Community |
| Advocacy | Referral/Advocacy | Social Proof, Community |

---

## MF5.8 Funnel Audience Framework

Maps Audience construction to funnel position, synthesizing Buying Stage (MF5.7.11) into concrete Audience-building guidance per stage. Each stage is documented with: Definition, Recommended Audience Types, Recommended Objective.

### MF5.8.1 TOF (Top of Funnel)

- **Definition:** Cold-prospecting Audience segments with no prior relationship to the Business (Awareness state, MF5.7.1).
- **Recommended Audience Types:** Core Audience (MF5.2.1), Advantage+ Audience (MF5.2.5), broad Lookalike tiers (MF5.5.10).
- **Recommended Objective:** Awareness (MF1.5.1), Traffic (MF1.5.2), Engagement (MF1.5.3) as an entry point.

### MF5.8.2 MOF (Middle of Funnel)

- **Definition:** Audience segments who have shown Interest or Intent (MF5.7.2–3) but not yet Purchase Readiness.
- **Recommended Audience Types:** Engaged Audience (MF5.2.6), Website Audience scoped to View Content/Add To Wishlist (MF5.2.7), tighter Lookalike tiers (MF5.5.5–7).
- **Recommended Objective:** Engagement (MF1.5.3), Leads (MF1.5.4), early-stage Sales nurture.

### MF5.8.3 BOF (Bottom of Funnel)

- **Definition:** Audience segments at Purchase Readiness or Decision Stage (MF5.7.4/12).
- **Recommended Audience Types:** Shopping Activity Custom Audience (MF5.4.14) scoped to Add To Cart/Initiate Checkout, Catalog Audience (MF5.2.16).
- **Recommended Objective:** Sales (MF1.5.6), Leads (MF1.5.4, Calls/Instant Form sub-types for high-consideration categories).

### MF5.8.4 Retention

- **Definition:** Existing-customer Audience segments (Loyalty state, MF5.7.13) targeted to sustain continued engagement/purchase behavior, distinct from acquisition-focused TOF/MOF/BOF stages.
- **Recommended Audience Types:** Customer List Audience (MF5.2.14) or repeat-Purchase Website Audience segments.
- **Recommended Objective:** Sales (repeat-purchase framing), Engagement (ongoing relationship maintenance).

### MF5.8.5 Upsell

- **Definition:** Existing-customer segments targeted with a higher-value product/tier than their current purchase history reflects.
- **Recommended Audience Types:** Customer List Audience (MF5.2.14) segmented by current product/tier, excluding those already on the higher tier.
- **Recommended Objective:** Sales, with Creative (MF4) emphasizing the incremental value of the higher tier.

### MF5.8.6 Cross-sell

- **Definition:** Existing-customer segments targeted with a complementary product/service distinct from their current purchase, rather than a higher tier of the same product (the Upsell distinction, MF5.8.5).
- **Recommended Audience Types:** Customer List Audience (MF5.2.14) segmented by purchased-product category, excluding those who already own the complementary product being advertised.
- **Recommended Objective:** Sales, Catalog Sales (MF3.5.13) specifically well-suited given its natural multi-product framing.

### MF5.8.7 Win-back

- **Definition:** Previously-active customer segments who have lapsed (no recent Purchase/engagement) targeted to re-engage them.
- **Recommended Audience Types:** Customer List Audience (MF5.2.14) or Website Audience (MF5.2.7) scoped to a Purchase Event with an *exclusion* of recent Purchase, isolating specifically lapsed customers.
- **Recommended Objective:** Sales, often paired with a specific win-back Offer (MF4.3.8) acknowledging the lapse.

### MF5.8.8 Loyalty (Funnel Stage View)

Cross-referenced from MF5.7.13 — as a Funnel Audience stage specifically, Loyalty-stage Audiences (distinct from the general Retention stage, MF5.8.4, in emphasizing relationship depth over transaction recency) are best targeted with Engagement-objective Creative reinforcing brand relationship (Community/Consistency principles, MF4.4.16/18) rather than a direct Sales ask on every touch.

### MF5.8.9 Referral

- **Definition:** Loyalty/Advocacy-tier segments (MF5.7.13–14) specifically targeted to encourage referring new customers, distinct from Advocacy's broader promotional behavior.
- **Recommended Audience Types:** High-Loyalty Customer List segments, ideally further filtered by known referral-program participation where tracked.
- **Recommended Objective:** Leads or Engagement, with Creative specifically framing the referral action/incentive.

### MF5.8.10 Advocacy (Funnel Stage View)
